metrics.title: FY2026 Guidance
metrics.label: ~45% YoY growth — Mounjaro +110% YoY in Q4 2025

metrics.title: Orforglipron FDA
metrics.label: Oral GLP-1 approval deadline — potentially March 28

metrics.title: Retatrutide Ph.2
metrics.label: Mean body weight loss at high dose — best-in-class pipeline

moat.description: Dominant GLP-1 franchise with patent protection into the late 2030s, reinforced by a deep clinical pipeline and manufacturing scale advantages.
moat.analysisSummary: Eli Lilly's moat is anchored in **scientific superiority and regulatory exclusivity** that cannot be replicated quickly:

analysisPoints.title: Tirzepatide Efficacy Superiority
analysisPoints.text: Mounjaro and Zepbound (tirzepatide) consistently outperform competing GLP-1 drugs in head-to-head efficacy data, with superior weight loss outcomes. This clinical evidence edge drives prescriber preference and formulary positioning that competitors must overcome with their own Phase 3 trial data — a multi-year barrier.

analysisPoints.title: Patent Wall Into the Late 2030s
analysisPoints.text: Lilly's CEO has confirmed tirzepatide compound patent protection extends 'into the back half of the 2030s' in major markets, contrasting sharply with Novo Nordisk's semaglutide facing patent expiry in select markets today. This 10+ year runway allows Lilly to compound its GLP-1 franchise revenues without near-term generic erosion.

analysisPoints.title: Manufacturing Moat at Scale
analysisPoints.text: Peptide manufacturing is extraordinarily complex; Lilly has invested billions into new manufacturing capacity globally. The expertise required to manufacture GLP-1 drugs at commercial scale — including sterile fill-finish and cold-chain logistics — represents a structural barrier that takes years for competitors to build.

growth.description: Q4 2025: $19.29B revenue (+42.6% YoY, beat) — Mounjaro $7.41B (+110%), Zepbound $4.26B (+123%). FY2026 guidance of $80–83B implies ~45% revenue growth on a $55B base. Orforglipron oral GLP-1 FDA decision by April 10 (potentially March 28), and retatrutide Phase 2 achieved 28.7% mean weight loss at high dose. The Hims & Hers / Novo Nordisk distribution deal (official launch today) intensifies oral competition in the telehealth channel but does not change tirzepatide's clinical superiority or LLY's patent wall into the late 2030s.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 90 (30%+ CAGR) + 5 oral GLP-1 TAM expansion (orforglipron FDA imminent, opens injection-resistant patients) + 5 retatrutide next-gen catalyst (28.7% Phase 2 weight loss) − 5 oral competition headwind (Novo Wegovy pill via Hims, Structure Therapeutics aleniglipron Phase 2 data) − 5 pricing risk (Medicare Part D negotiation risk) = 90

drivers.name: Zepbound (obesity)
drivers.metric: $4.26B Q4 2025 (+123% YoY), launched Jan 2024 — still ramping

drivers.name: Orforglipron + Retatrutide
drivers.metric: FDA decision Apr 10; retatrutide 28.7% Phase 2 weight loss, Phase 3 ongoing

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If Hims & Hers / Novo distribution partnership shifts 15%+ of new GLP-1 prescriptions to oral semaglutide before orforglipron approval, AND Structure Therapeutics' aleniglipron Phase 3 data outperforms orforglipron in 2027, LLY's oral market share erodes below 30% and consensus EPS estimates for 2028 decline 15%+

valuation.description: At ~$937 — down 13% YTD and 17% from the 52-week high of $1,132 — LLY trades at a widening discount to the $1,200 base case. The pullback reflects oral competition fears (Hims/Novo deal) and orforglipron FDA uncertainty, not fundamental impairment of a company guiding $80–83B revenue for 2026 (~45% growth). Analyst consensus sits at $1,217.

peAnalysis.summary: At 33× forward P/E with a PEG of 0.7×, LLY is significantly cheaper than its pharma peer average (~45×) and even cheaper than the broad healthcare sector (~28× on much lower growth). A PEG under 1.0 on 45% EPS growth is rare in large-cap pharma and typically signals a mispriced growth story — the oral competition fears driving the 13% YTD decline are creating an attractive entry. The wide gap between the 49× trailing and 33× forward P/E reflects accelerating EPS, not compression.

bear.description: Oral GLP-1 competition accelerates via the Hims/Novo telehealth channel while pricing reform erodes net revenue, and a pipeline setback triggers multiple compression.
points: Hims & Hers / Novo Nordisk distribution partnership captures 20%+ of new GLP-1 telehealth prescriptions for oral Wegovy before orforglipron launches; Structure Therapeutics' aleniglipron Phase 3 outperforms orforglipron, fragmenting LLY's oral market share below 25%
U.S. Medicare/Medicaid Part D GLP-1 coverage price negotiations cut net pricing by 25%+ before 2028, compressing revenue growth below 15% and pushing FY2027 revenue to ~$90B vs. consensus $110B+

Retatrutide Phase 3 trial fails to replicate the 28.7% Phase 2 weight-loss result — settling at ~20% mean loss — eliminating the 'best-in-class next-gen' premium and leaving LLY without a differentiated asset beyond Zepbound

base.description: FDA approves orforglipron by April 10, LLY negotiates comparable telehealth distribution to offset the Hims/Novo deal, and retatrutide Phase 3 confirms superiority — restoring the premium multiple.
points: FDA approves orforglipron oral GLP-1 pill by April 10, 2026; LLY negotiates telehealth channel deals with Hims or rival platforms within 60 days, capturing 30%+ of the oral GLP-1 prescribing market
Retatrutide Phase 3 confirms 22%+ mean body weight loss (vs. Wegovy ~15%), FDA approval by late 2026 — establishing Lilly as the only company with best-in-class oral and injectable next-gen GLP-1s
Donanemab (Alzheimer's) reaches $2B+ annual revenue by 2027, and FY2026 revenue closes at the high end of the $80–83B guidance range, with EPS of $28–30

bull.description: Retatrutide becomes best-in-class weight-loss drug with 25%+ body weight reduction, oral GLP-1 captures mainstream adoption, and Alzheimer's franchise reaches $5B+.
points: Retatrutide achieves 25%+ mean body weight loss in Phase 3, approved by FDA in 2027 and generating $15B+ in annual revenue by 2029
Orforglipron oral pill penetrates 30% of obesity drug market within 3 years of launch, doubling the addressable patient pool vs. injection-only therapies
Donanemab Alzheimer's franchise and oncology pipeline (e.g., LOXO-305) collectively reach $8B+ revenue by 2028, reducing GLP-1 concentration risk below 50%

learnedInterfaces.note: N/A — physicians prescribe drugs based on clinical evidence and patient outcomes, not UI familiarity; interface learning costs do not apply to pharmaceuticals.

businessLogic.note: Lilly's GLP-1 peptide synthesis, formulation IP, and cold-chain manufacturing know-how represent complex proprietary processes that competitors cannot decode from reverse engineering alone.

publicDataAccess.note: N/A — Lilly's competitive moat does not rely on controlling access to a public data source; not applicable to the pharmaceutical business model.

talentScarcity.note: World-class endocrinologists, peptide chemists, and regulatory affairs specialists are scarce globally; Lilly's 140-year reputation and compensation attract the best pharma talent disproportionately.

bundling.note: N/A — pharmaceutical drugs are not sold in software-style bundles; individual drug approvals and prescriptions are the unit of sale.

proprietaryData.note: Lilly holds decades of real-world clinical trial data for tirzepatide across thousands of patients — outcomes data that grows with every prescription and feeds future drug development and label expansions.

regulatoryLockIn.note: FDA approvals, EU and global regulatory clearances, GMP manufacturing facility certifications, and payer formulary placements create compounding switching barriers that take years to displace.

networkEffects.note: Growing physician experience with tirzepatide creates a prescribing flywheel — more patient success stories build physician confidence and peer recommendations, reinforcing Lilly's market share without additional marketing spend.

transactionEmbedding.note: Mounjaro and Zepbound are embedded in major pharmacy benefit formularies, employer health plans, and Medicare Part D — removing them faces enormous patient, physician, and political resistance.

systemOfRecord.note: N/A — Lilly is a drug manufacturer, not a system-of-record platform; this moat type does not apply to the pharmaceutical industry.

tenMoats.verdict: Lilly's core moat — patent-protected biologics with regulatory approval — is structurally immune to AI disruption, and AI drug discovery tools actually accelerate Lilly's pipeline advantage by compressing early-stage discovery timelines. The company is a net beneficiary of AI adoption, using it to identify novel targets and optimize clinical trials faster than underfunded competitors.
